title: Laundry stain spray
subtitle: A pre-treatment stain spray with washing soda and castile soap
excerpt: A laundry pre-treatment spray made from washing soda, castile liquid soap, and distilled water. Spray directly onto stains before loading the wash. The alkaline washing soda lifts protein, grease, and tannin stains. Effective on grass, food, and light oil marks. Fill into a 250 ml spray bottle.
sourceNotes: Dri-Pak technical notes on washing soda in laundry applications (dri-pak.co.uk, accessed May 2026). Castile soap as a surfactant in alkaline stain removal.

content.text: spray applies concentrated cleaning chemistry directly to a stain before the main wash dilutes everything. This spray uses

as the main active ingredient, with castile soap to boost surfactant action. It works best on protein stains (grass, blood, egg), tannin stains (tea, coffee, red wine), and light grease. For heavy grease or oil, additional treatment may be needed.

Wear protective gloves when dissolving the washing soda. Do not use on wool or silk, which are damaged by strong alkalis. Test on a hidden seam of any coloured or delicate fabric before applying to a visible area.

items.name: Washing soda
items.prepNote: Sodium carbonate. Dissolve fully in the warm water before adding the soap. Wear protective gloves.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: Warm enough to dissolve the washing soda easily. Allow to cool before adding the soap.

items.name: Castile liquid soap
items.prepNote: Concentrated castile soap. Add after the washing soda is dissolved and the water has cooled; hot water can denature the soap proteins.

content.text: Wearing protective gloves, dissolve the washing soda in warm distilled water, stirring until fully clear. Allow to cool to room temperature before adding the soap; adding castile soap to a hot, highly alkaline solution can cause it to split.
Add the castile liquid soap and stir gently to avoid excessive foaming. Using the funnel, pour into the spray bottle. Label with the making date.

Spray directly onto the stain and work into the fabric with a fingernail or soft brush. Leave for at least 5 minutes, then wash in the machine as normal. For older or set stains, leave for up to 30 minutes. Shake the bottle before each use.

Store in the spray bottle away from direct sunlight. Shake before each use; the castile soap can separate from the alkaline solution over time. Keeps for 3 months. Discard if a foul smell develops or if the spray becomes cloudy in a way that does not clear on shaking.
